About the Department

MENU

Our department is a four-year undergraduate program listed in the "Higher Education Programs and Quotas Guide" with the

Equal Weight (EA)

score type. Admitted students begin their four-year academic education directly in their first year.

Our four-year curriculum is meticulously designed to ensure our students grow in line with our global aviation vision, focusing on the

managerial competencies

and

technical operational processes

required by the industry.

Core Focus Areas of Our Curriculum:

The general content of our courses covering aviation management and strategic operational processes includes:

Aviation Management:

Introduction to Civil      Aviation, Airline Management, Airport Management.

Operational Processes:

Ground Handling Management,      Fundamentals of Air Traffic Management.

Safety and Security:

Aviation Safety and Aviation      Security.

Logistics and Economics:

Air Cargo Management, Aviation      Economics.

Legislation and Language:

Aviation Law, Aviation English      (Sectoral Terminology).

Additionally, courses such as

Customer Relationship Management (CRM)

and our faculty-wide

Entrepreneurship

course enable our students to gain professional competence in the field of management sciences.
